interaction_models.goal: Set ticket availability limits without exceeding the total physical space of the venue.
interaction_models.user_mental_model: All tickets share the same physical venue space. I want to bundle some tickets together while tracking total attendees.
interaction_models.system_model: Hierarchical capacity validation. Venue capacity serves as the absolute ceiling. Ticket quantities are tracked individually or as part of a shared stock pool. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.

core_entities.name: Shared Capacity Pool
core_entities.purpose: Group multiple ticket types under a single limit

interaction_flow.system_response: Renders a capacity progress bar indicating 0 / [Value]. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
interaction_flow.visible_feedback: Visual bar appears at the top of the interface.

interaction_flow.user_action: Drags Ticket row onto another Ticket row
interaction_flow.system_response: Creates a new Shared Capacity Pool containing both tickets. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
interaction_flow.visible_feedback: Rows snap together into a grouped container with a new 'Shared Limit' input field.

interaction_flow.system_response: Updates the global capacity progress bar. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
interaction_flow.visible_feedback: Progress bar fills proportionally. Numerical ratio updates.

decision_points.decision_description: Determine if specific ticket types should share a stock limit

automation_opportunities: Dynamically calculate and display remaining unallocated venue capacity.
Auto-group tickets into a default pool if no individual limits are specified.
edge_cases: Sum of individual ticket limits and pool limits exceeds venue capacity.
User attempts to delete a pool that already has sold tickets.

failure_states.cause: Total configured limits exceed venue capacity
failure_states.system_behavior: Blocks publishing and flags the overage. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
failure_states.user_feedback: Capacity bar turns red. Tooltip explains by how much the limit is exceeded.

success_feedback: Real-time visual update of the capacity bar remaining within safe thresholds.

complexity_score.notes: System must evaluate capacity math instantaneously during user input.

interaction_models.goal: Configure and verify scheduled price changes or quantity-based price tiers.
interaction_models.user_mental_model: I want to see a timeline of how much a ticket costs on any given day or after a certain number of sales.
interaction_models.system_model: Chronological array of pricing rules. The system evaluates the current date or current sales volume against the array to output the active price. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.

core_entities.name: Pricing Rule
core_entities.purpose: Define a condition and the resulting price

core_entities.name: Timeline Data
core_entities.purpose: Data structure for rendering the visual chart

interaction_flow.system_response: Opens input fields for trigger condition and price. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
interaction_flow.visible_feedback: New row appears in configuration modal.

interaction_flow.system_response: Plots the new point on the horizontal timeline chart. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
interaction_flow.visible_feedback: Chart updates immediately, showing the old price trajectory changing to the new price at the specified date.
interaction_flow.state_change: PricingRule populated and added to Timeline Data

decision_points.decision_description: Choose between time-based or volume-based price changes

automation_opportunities: Sort rules chronologically automatically upon entry.
Suggest standard 'Early Bird' intervals based on event date.
edge_cases: Two rules configured for the exact same date or sales threshold.
A date rule is set after the event start date.

failure_states.cause: Chronological gap or invalid sequence (e.g., setting a date backwards)
failure_states.system_behavior: Highlights the overlapping or illogical rule. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
failure_states.user_feedback: The timeline visually displays a red warning icon at the conflict point; conflicting inputs are outlined in red.

success_feedback: Smooth rendering of the yield curve visually confirming the strategy.

complexity_score.notes: Reduces cognitive load by offloading timeline visualization to the system.

interaction_models.goal: Create promotional strategies using codes, links, or referrals in one place.
interaction_models.user_mental_model: I want to give someone special access or a discount using a code or link.
interaction_models.system_model: A unified rules engine evaluating Trigger inputs against configured Conditions to output a Result modifier. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.

core_entities.name: Trigger
core_entities.purpose: The user input or context that activates the campaign

core_entities.name: Result
core_entities.purpose: The modification applied to the checkout state

interaction_flow.system_response: Displays Trigger and Result selection interface. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
interaction_flow.visible_feedback: Dropdown menus for 'When attendee uses...' and 'They receive...'

interaction_flow.system_response: Validates code uniqueness. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
interaction_flow.visible_feedback: Green checkmark next to code input.

interaction_flow.user_action: Selects Result: 'Reveal hidden ticket' and selects 'Backstage Pass'
interaction_flow.system_response: Links the target ticket visibility state to this campaign. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
interaction_flow.visible_feedback: Summary text reads: 'Code VIP10 will reveal Backstage Pass'.

decision_points.decision_description: Define the combination of trigger and result

automation_opportunities: Auto-generate random alphanumeric strings for promo codes.
Automatically append URL parameters for sharing based on campaign name.
edge_cases: A campaign targets a ticket type that is sold out.

failure_states.cause: Duplicate trigger value used across different campaigns
failure_states.system_behavior: Rejects the trigger input. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
failure_states.user_feedback: Error message: 'This code is already in use by another campaign.'

success_feedback: Clear, plain-English summary sentence of the configured campaign logic.

complexity_score.notes: Simplifies multiple disparate marketing tools into a single logic flow.

interaction_models.goal: Set checkout questions for all tickets, altering them only when a specific ticket requires it.
interaction_models.user_mental_model: I need everyone's name and email, but I only need a t-shirt size for the VIP pass.
interaction_models.system_model: Parent configuration object applies default properties to children. Children evaluate an override boolean to determine inheritance. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.

interaction_flow.user_action: Sets 'Require Phone Number' at Event Level
interaction_flow.system_response: Updates global defaults; applies to all child tickets without explicit overrides. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.

interaction_flow.user_action: Opens individual Ticket settings and toggles 'Custom Data Collection'
interaction_flow.system_response: Breaks inheritance for this specific ticket and displays local configuration options. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
interaction_flow.visible_feedback: Expands a panel pre-filled with the event defaults, allowing modification.

interaction_flow.user_action: Adds 'T-Shirt Size' question to the specific ticket
interaction_flow.system_response: Saves local override. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
interaction_flow.visible_feedback: Question appears in the local list.

decision_points.decision_description: Decide if a ticket needs unique information collected at checkout

automation_opportunities: Pre-fill ticket overrides with event defaults so the user doesn't start from scratch when toggling the override.
edge_cases: User modifies Event Defaults after setting up custom Ticket Overrides.

failure_states.cause: Conflicting data types in custom questions
failure_states.system_behavior: Validates field types. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
failure_states.user_feedback: Displays format validation error on the specific field.

success_feedback: A badge on the ticket row indicating 'Custom Checkout Rules Applied'.

interaction_models.goal: Build the core event quickly without being blocked by administrative setup.
interaction_models.user_mental_model: I want to see what my event page looks like before I spend 20 minutes finding my bank details.
interaction_models.system_model: State machine separating draft viability from publishing validation. Validation schemas are conditionally applied based on the requested transition state. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.

interaction_flow.user_action: Completes basic event and ticket details in wizard
interaction_flow.system_response: Saves data and generates a usable draft preview. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
interaction_flow.visible_feedback: Navigates to Event Dashboard; status shows 'Draft'.

interaction_flow.system_response: Evaluates Pre-Flight Checklist requirements. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
interaction_flow.visible_feedback: Opens a modal listing pending administrative tasks (e.g., 'Add Bank Account').

interaction_flow.user_action: Completes missing bank details from the modal
interaction_flow.system_response: Updates checklist status and executes state transition to Published. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
interaction_flow.visible_feedback: Task is marked complete; modal closes; status updates to 'Published' with a live link.

decision_points.is_information_available: Might require user to look it up

automation_opportunities: Reuse bank and organizer details from previously published events.
edge_cases: User abandons the flow at the Pre-Flight check.
External verification service (e.g., KYC) is delayed.

failure_states.cause: Attempting to publish with incomplete administrative data
failure_states.system_behavior: Blocks state transition to Published. [Inference] This is expected behavior, not guaranteed.
failure_states.user_feedback: Highlights missing mandatory fields in the Pre-Flight Checklist.

success_feedback: Confetti animation or distinct 'Live' status badge upon successful publication.

complexity_score.notes: Isolates the highest cognitive load tasks to the very end of the user journey, increasing upfront conversion.
